fact: The Britannic was the Titanic's sister ship and sank in 1916
source: Britannic: Patroness of the Mediterranean, Simon Mills (2000); also Wikipedia

fact: Design improvements after Titanic included a double hull, larger lifeboats, and improved watertight bulkheads
source: Britannic: Patroness of the Mediterranean, Simon Mills (2000); also Encyclopedia Titanica

fact: Open portholes allowed water to flood lower decks, accelerating the sinking
source: Widely documented; detailed in Simon Mills' research and confirmed by Robert Ballard's 1995 expedition

fact: Lifeboats launched prematurely were dragged into the still-spinning propellers, killing occupants
source: Britannic: Patroness of the Mediterranean, Simon Mills (2000); survivor testimony from nurse Violet Jessop

fact: The Britannic sank in approximately 55 minutes
source: Britannic: Patroness of the Mediterranean, Simon Mills (2000); widely documented as roughly 55 minutes from mine strike at 8:12 AM to sinking at ~9:07 AM

fact: Thirty people died in the sinking of the Britannic
source: Widely documented; of 1,066 aboard, 30 were killed — mostly from the lifeboat/propeller incidents. Figure cited by Simon Mills and multiple naval history sources.
